title: React Native Styling Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Flexbox, the StyleSheet API, and Reanimated.

questions.question: How do developers predominantly apply styling in React Native, marking a massive departure from traditional web development?

options.text: By writing pure HTML inside the components.
By importing external `.css` or `.scss` files globally at the root.
By defining CamelCase JavaScript objects using the `StyleSheet.create()` API and passing them seamlessly via the `style` prop.
By utilizing Tailwind CSS classes exclusively.

questions.question: What layout engine is fundamentally mandatory for positioning UI elements across all React Native applications globally?

options.text: Absolute Pixel Positioning
Flexbox (via the Yoga engine).
CSS Grid Frameworks
HTML Tables

questions.question: Regarding the React Native implementation of Flexbox, what is the single biggest difference compared exactly against standard Web CSS?

options.text: It does not support `justifyContent`.
It throws errors if `alignItems` is applied.
The default `flexDirection` is `column` (top-to-bottom) rather than `row` (left-to-right).
It uses `display: inline` by default.

questions.question: Why did the community adopt the 'React Native Reanimated' library as the industry standard for executing complex mobile animations?

options.text: Because it acts as a wrapper around the physical HTML canvas tag.
Because it physically runs the animation exclusively on the native UI thread, guaranteeing a perfectly smooth 60fps experience even if the Javascript thread completely freezes while processing data.
Because it is required to bypass the Apple App Store.
Because it automatically converts PNGs into Vector Graphics.

questions.question: If a mobile designer builds a breathtakingly complex loading animation using Adobe After Effects, what is the best strategy for a developer to implement it effortlessly in React Native?

options.text: By meticulously rewriting the entire animation exactly in raw Javascript code line-by-line.
By exporting the robust Adobe animation flawlessly as a tiny JSON file utilizing 'Lottie' and rendering the JSON directly in the app natively.
By recording the animation as an enormous `.mp4` video and looping it.
By generating a thousand `.png` frames and cycling through them manually.
